qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default public Oxia service permits unauthenticated read and write access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing authentication on the public gRPC dataserver startup path, while the advisory concerns CA bundle parsing in common/security/tls.go::trustedCertPool() during TLS certificate-chain validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's TLS CA bundle parsing flaw. The session's only real qualifying finding is a separate unauthenticated public-service exposure in Oxia's gRPC dataserver startup and RPC authentication path.
evidence: GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6 describes common/security/tls.go::trustedCertPool() decoding only the first PEM block from trustedCaFile and silently accepting empty or invalid CA pools.
The upstream fix from v0.16.1 to v0.16.2 changes trustedCertPool() to iterate over all PEM blocks, accept only CERTIFICATE blocks, and return an error when no valid certificates are found.

default_unauthenticated_public_service_access/vulnerability_report.json instead traces exploitation through cmd/standalone/cmd.go, cmd/server/cmd.go, oxiad/common/rpc/auth/authentication.go, oxiad/common/rpc/grpc_server.go, and oxiad/dataserver/public_rpc_server.go.
That finding's demonstrated impact is unauthenticated read and write access on the public gRPC service, not failed mTLS certificate-chain validation for multi-certificate PEM bundles.
